name: ctfr
summary:
CTFR
Do you miss AXFR technique? This tool allows to get the subdomains from a HTTP**S** website in a few seconds.  
How it works? CTFR does not use neither dictionary attack nor brute-force, it just abuses of Certificate Transparency logs.  
For more information about CT logs, check www.certificate-transparency.org and [crt.sh](https://crt.sh/).

documentation.readme: CTFR Do you miss AXFR technique? This tool allows to get the subdomains from a HTTPS website in a few seconds. How it works? CTFR does not use neither dictionary attack nor brute-force, it just abuses of Certificate Transparency logs. For more information about CT logs, check www.certificate-transparency.org and crt.sh.
usage_sections: Getting Started: Please, follow the instructions below for installing and run CTFR.

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: Extra HTTP header in key:value form (repeatable).

arguments.name: --insecure
arguments.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (lab use only).

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Emit JSON output instead of human-readable text.

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

function_signatures.name: scan
function_signatures.description: Perform a read-only scan / probe of the target.

function_signatures.name: exploit
function_signatures.description: Run the offensive step against the target. Returns ok, data, error envelope.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: git clone https://github.com/UnaPibaGeek/ctfr.git; pip3 install -r requirements.txt. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `Python 3.0 or later.`. Further invocations from upstream: `pip3 (sudo apt-get install python3-pip).`; `python3 ctfr.py --help`. CTFR Do you miss AXFR technique? This tool allows to get the subdomains from a HTTPS website in a few seconds. How it works? CTFR does not use neither dictionary attack nor brute-force, it just abuses of Certificate Transparency logs. For more information about CT logs, check www.certificate-transparency.org and crt.sh.
